776	/-! ### L2 `q_*`-coherence chain: push–pull turns a coproduct into a product
777	
778	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
779	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
780	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of
781	the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q` is, up to the canonical leg iso
782	`pushPullCoprodLegIso`, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit.  This lets the
783	canonical comparison `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` be matched against
784	the manifestly-iso chain through `coprodDecompMap`. -/
785	
786	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where
787	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
788	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
789	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
790	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
791	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
792	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
793	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
794	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
795	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso
796	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≪≫
797	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
798	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
799	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)
800	
801	-- The final `congr 1` discharges the proof-irrelevant `eqToHom` over-triangle transports against
802	-- concrete pushforward/pullback objects, whose `whnf` exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
803	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
804	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
805	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
806	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
807	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
808	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
809	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
810	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
811	      (pushforward q).map
812	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
813	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
814	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
815	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
816	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
817	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
818	        (pushforward c).map
819	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
820	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
821	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
822	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
823	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
824	  subst wC
825	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
826	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
827	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
828	  rw [← hLAU]
829	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl
830	
831	end BinaryDecomp
832	
833	/-- Push–pull on a binary coproduct of two legs is the binary product of the two leg push–pulls.
834	The forward map is the canonical `prod.lift` of the two push–pull maps of the coproduct inclusions
835	(the mandatory framing the downstream section-identification needs); it is shown to be an
836	isomorphism by matching it leg-by-leg, via the per-leg coherence
837	`pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★), against the manifestly-invertible reference chain through the
838	binary disjoint-union decomposition `coprodDecompMap`.  Project-local L2 assembly
839	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_binary_coprod_prod`). -/
840	noncomputable def pushPull_binary_coprod_prod (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
841	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)) ≅
842	      pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ := by
843	  set q : Y₀.left ⨿ Y₁.left ⟶ X := Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom with hq
844	  set M := (Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F with hM
845	  have wInl : (Limits.coprod.inl : Y₀.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₀.hom := Limits.coprod.inl_desc _ _
846	  have wInr : (Limits.coprod.inr : Y₁.left ⟶ _) ≫ q = Y₁.hom := Limits.coprod.inr_desc _ _
847	  set overInl : Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl wInl with hoverInl
848	  set overInr : Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk q := Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr wInr with hoverInr
849	  haveI : IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := isIso_coprodDecompMap M
850	  -- The per-leg identifications.  Their codomains are pinned to `pushPullObj F Y₀`/`Y₁`
851	  -- (defeq to the `pushPullObj F (Over.mk Y₀.hom)` produced by `pushPullCoprodLegIso`); the
852	  -- syntactic pin is essential so the `Category.assoc`/`prod.map_fst` rewrites below can match
853	  -- the trailing `prod.fst` on `pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁`.
854	  set idiso₀ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inl).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inl))
855	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ :=
856	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F with hidiso0
857	  set idiso₁ : (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward Limits.coprod.inr).obj (M.restrict Limits.coprod.inr))
858	      ≅ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
859	    pushPullCoprodLegIso q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F with hidiso1
860	  have hcoh0 : pushPullMap F overInl
861	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inl).unit.app M)
862	          ≫ idiso₀.hom := by
863	    rw [hidiso0]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inl Y₀.hom wInl F
864	  have hcoh1 : pushPullMap F overInr
865	      = (pushforward q).map ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction Limits.coprod.inr).unit.app M)
866	          ≫ idiso₁.hom := by
867	    rw [hidiso1]; exact pushPull_binary_leg_coherence q Limits.coprod.inr Y₁.hom wInr F
868	  set chainIso : (pushforward q).obj M ≅ pushPullObj F Y₀ ⨯ pushPullObj F Y₁ :=
869	    (pushforward q).mapIso (asIso (coprodDecompMap M)) ≪≫
870	      Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso (pushforward q) _ _ ≪≫
871	      Limits.prod.mapIso idiso₀ idiso₁ with hchain
872	  -- Match the canonical comparison against the reference chain entirely through `prod.lift`
873	  -- identities (`prod.lift_map`, `prod.comp_lift`, and `prodComparison = prod.lift (q_* fst)
874	  -- (q_* snd)`), avoiding any `≫ prod.fst` projection that the surrounding pushforward objects
875	  -- make awkward to reassociate.
876	  have hcmp : Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr) = chainIso.hom := by
877	    rw [hcoh0, hcoh1, hchain, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, asIso_hom,
878	      Limits.prod.mapIso_hom, Limits.PreservesLimitPair.iso_hom]
879	    show Limits.prod.lift _ _ =
880	      (pushforward q).map (coprodDecompMap M) ≫
881	        Limits.prod.lift ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.fst) ((pushforward q).map Limits.prod.snd)
882	          ≫ Limits.prod.map idiso₀.hom idiso₁.hom
883	    rw [Limits.prod.lift_map, Limits.prod.comp_lift, ← Functor.map_comp_assoc,
884	      ← Functor.map_comp_assoc, coprodDecompMap, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.prod.lift_snd]
885	    rfl
886	  haveI : IsIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)) := by
887	    rw [hcmp]; infer_instance
888	  exact asIso (Limits.prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr))

890	/-- The forward map of `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` in its canonical `prod.lift`-of-push–pull-maps
891	form (the binary case of the canonical framing kept through the finite induction). -/
892	private lemma pushPull_binary_coprod_prod_hom (F : X.Modules) (Y₀ Y₁ : Over X) :
893	    (pushPull_binary_coprod_prod F Y₀ Y₁).hom =
894	      Limits.prod.lift
895	        (pushPullMap F (Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inl (Limits.coprod.inl_desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom) :
896	          Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom)))
897	        (pushPullMap F (Over.homMk Limits.coprod.inr (Limits.coprod.inr_desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom) :
898	          Y₁ ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc Y₀.hom Y₁.hom))) := rfl

900	/-! ### Finite-index induction: push–pull turns a finite coproduct into a finite product
901	
902	The binary `pushPull_binary_coprod_prod` is upgraded to an arbitrary finite index `ι` by
903	`Finite.induction_empty_option`.  The chain runs through four leaves:
904	* `pushPullObjCongr` — transport a push–pull object along a slice iso;
905	* `overSigmaOptionIso` — slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split;
906	* `piOptionIso` — the dual `Option`-product split;
907	* `pushPull_coprod_prod_empty` — the empty base case.
908	-/
909	
910	/-- Push–pull objects transport along a slice isomorphism `e : Y ≅ Y'` in `Over X`.  The
911	push–pull object is a contravariant functor of its slice argument, so the forward map is
912	`pushPullMap F e.inv` and the backward map `pushPullMap F e.hom`.  Project-local
913	(blueprint `lem:pushPullObjCongr`). -/
914	noncomputable def pushPullObjCongr (F : X.Modules) {Y Y' : Over X} (e : Y ≅ Y') :
915	    pushPullObj F Y ≅ pushPullObj F Y' where
916	  hom := pushPullMap F e.inv
917	  inv := pushPullMap F e.hom
918	  hom_inv_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.hom_inv_id, pushPullMap_id]
919	  inv_hom_id := by rw [← pushPullMap_comp, e.inv_hom_id, pushPullMap_id]
920	
921	/-- Slice lift of the `Option`-coproduct split (`sigmaOptionIso`): for a family of slice
922	objects `legs : Option α → Over X`, the coproduct slice object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc (·.hom))`
923	is isomorphic to the binary-split slice object.  Built as `Over.isoMk` of the underlying
924	`sigmaOptionIso` together with the structure-map compatibility.  Project-local
925	(blueprint `lem:over_sigmaOptionIso`). -/
926	noncomputable def overSigmaOptionIso {α : Type*} (legs : Option α → Over X)
927	    [HasCoproduct (fun o => (legs o).left)]
928	    [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)]
929	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (legs none).left (∐ fun a : α => (legs (some a)).left)] :
930	    Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun o => (legs o).hom)) ≅
931	      Over.mk (Limits.coprod.desc (legs none).hom
932	        (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a : α => (legs (some a)).hom))) :=
933	  Over.isoMk (sigmaOptionIso (fun o => (legs o).left)) (by
934	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun o => ?_)
935	    rcases o with _ | a
936	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom,
937	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
938	    · simp only [sigmaOptionIso, Over.mk_left, Over.mk_hom, Category.assoc,
939	        Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
940	
941	/-- The dual `Option`-product split: for `W : Option α → C` the total product splits off its
942	`none` factor.  Project-local (blueprint `lem:piOptionIso`). -/
943	noncomputable def piOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (W : Option α → C)
944	    [HasProduct W] [HasProduct (fun a : α => W (some a))]
945	    [HasBinaryProduct (W none) (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a))] :
946	    (∏ᶜ W) ≅ W none ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun a : α => W (some a)) where
947	  hom := Limits.prod.lift (Limits.Pi.π W none)
948	    (Limits.Pi.lift (fun a => Limits.Pi.π W (some a)))
949	  inv := Limits.Pi.lift (fun o => Option.rec Limits.prod.fst
950	    (fun a => Limits.prod.snd ≫ Limits.Pi.π (fun a : α => W (some a)) a) o)
951	  hom_inv_id := by
952	    apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
953	    rintro (_ | a)
954	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Category.id_comp]
955	    · rw [Category.id_comp, Category.assoc, Limits.Pi.lift_π, ← Category.assoc,
956	        Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
957	  inv_hom_id := by
958	    apply Limits.prod.hom_ext
959	    · simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_fst, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
960	    · apply Limits.Pi.hom_ext
961	      intro a
962	      simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.prod.lift_snd, Limits.Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp]
963	
964	/-- The coproduct inclusion of leg `i`, viewed as an over-morphism into the descent object
965	`Over.mk (Sigma.desc (·.hom))`. -/
966	noncomputable def coprodOverIncl {ι : Type*} (legs : ι → Over X)
967	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)] (i : ι) :
968	    legs i ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom)) :=

968	    legs i ⟶ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom)) :=
969	  Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι (fun i => (legs i).left) i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
970	
971	/-- The canonical comparison map from the push–pull object on the coproduct of the legs to
972	the product of the per-leg push–pull objects: the `Pi.lift` of the push–pull maps of the
973	coproduct inclusions.  This is the canonical framing kept throughout the finite induction. -/
974	noncomputable def coprodToProdMap {ι : Type*} (F : X.Modules) (legs : ι → Over X)
975	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => (legs i).left)]
976	    [HasProduct (fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i))] :
977	    pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (legs i).hom))) ⟶
978	      ∏ᶜ fun i => pushPullObj F (legs i) :=
979	  Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl legs i))
980	
981	/-- A sheaf of modules over an empty (initial) scheme is a zero object.  All its sections
982	vanish: the structure sheaf has subsingleton sections over every open (`IsEmpty ↥Z`), so every
983	module of sections is subsingleton, and the underlying abelian presheaf is pointwise zero.  We
984	reflect `𝟙 = 0` through the faithful `toPresheaf`. -/
985	private lemma isZero_modules_of_isEmpty {Z : Scheme} [IsEmpty ↥Z] (M : Z.Modules) :
986	    IsZero M := by
987	  rw [IsZero.iff_id_eq_zero]
988	  apply (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).map_injective
989	  rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id, (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).map_zero]
990	  ext U x
991	  haveI : Subsingleton ↑(((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf Z).obj M).obj (op U)) := by
992	    have h : Subsingleton ↑Γ(M, U) := Module.subsingleton ↑Γ(Z, U) _
993	    exact h
994	  exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
995	
996	/-- Empty base case of the finite induction: for the empty index the comparison map
997	`coprodToProdMap` is an isomorphism (both source and target are terminal — the push–pull
998	of the initial scheme and the empty product).  Project-local
999	(blueprint `lem:pushPull_coprod_prod_empty`). -/
1000	private theorem pushPull_coprod_prod_empty (F : X.Modules) (legs : PEmpty.{u + 1} → Over X) :
1001	    IsIso (coprodToProdMap F legs) := by
1002	  -- Target: the empty product is terminal.
1003	  have hY : Limits.IsTerminal (∏ᶜ fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => pushPullObj F (legs i)) :=
1004	    Limits.IsTerminal.ofUniqueHom (fun _ => Limits.Pi.lift (fun i => i.elim))
1005	      (fun _ m => Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => i.elim))
1006	  -- Source: push–pull of the initial scheme.  `pushforward q` is a right adjoint, so it
1007	  -- preserves the terminal object; and `(pullback q).obj F` is terminal over the initial
1008	  -- scheme (its structure sheaf has zero sections, so every module sheaf is terminal).
1009	  have hX : Limits.IsTerminal (pushPullObj F
1010	      (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).hom)))) := by
1011	    -- `pushPullObj F Y = (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((pullback Y.hom).obj F)`; pushforward preserves
1012	    -- zero objects (it is additive), and the pulled-back module lives over the initial scheme
1013	    -- `∐ PEmpty`, whose only sheaf of modules is zero.
1014	    refine (CategoryTheory.Functor.map_isZero (Scheme.Modules.pushforward _) ?_).isTerminal
1015	    -- The base scheme `∐ PEmpty` is the initial scheme, hence has empty carrier; every sheaf of
1016	    -- modules over it is a zero object.
1017	    haveI : IsEmpty ↥((Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).hom)).left) := by
1018	      rw [← AlgebraicGeometry.isInitial_iff_isEmpty]
1019	      exact ⟨Limits.isColimitEquivIsInitialOfIsEmpty Scheme _
1020	        (Limits.colimit.isColimit (Discrete.functor (fun i : PEmpty.{u + 1} => (legs i).left)))⟩
1021	    exact isZero_modules_of_isEmpty _
1022	  exact Limits.isIso_of_isTerminal hX hY _
1023	
1024	/-- Reindexing step of the finite induction: the comparison map's iso-status transports
1025	along an equivalence `e : α ≃ β` of index types.  Project-local. -/
1026	private theorem coprodToProd_isIso_of_equiv (F : X.Modules) {α β : Type u} (e : α ≃ β)
1027	    (ih : ∀ (legs : α → Over X), IsIso (coprodToProdMap F legs))
1028	    (legs : β → Over X) : IsIso (coprodToProdMap F legs) := by
1029	  -- The induction hypothesis gives iso-status for the `α`-reindexed family `legs ∘ e`.
1030	  haveI := ih (fun a => legs (e a))
1031	  -- Source coproduct reindex (orientation chosen so the factor isos are literally `Iso.refl`):
1032	  -- `∐_α (legs∘e).left ≅ ∐_β legs.left`.
1033	  let u : (∐ fun a => (legs (e a)).left) ≅ (∐ fun b => (legs b).left) :=
1034	    Sigma.whiskerEquiv (f := fun a => (legs (e a)).left) (g := fun b => (legs b).left)
1035	      e (fun a => Iso.refl _)
1036	  have hw : u.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun b => (legs b).hom)
1037	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => (legs (e a)).hom) := by
1038	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun a => ?_)
1039	    simp only [u, Sigma.whiskerEquiv, Iso.refl_inv, Limits.Sigma.ι_comp_map'_assoc,
1040	      Category.id_comp, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
1041	  -- Slice iso between the two descent objects in `Over X`.
1042	  let mIso : (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => (legs (e a)).hom)) : Over X)
1043	      ≅ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun b => (legs b).hom)) := Over.isoMk u hw
1044	  -- `pushPullMap F mIso.hom` is the inverse leg of `pushPullObjCongr`, hence an isomorphism.
1045	  haveI : IsIso (pushPullMap F mIso.hom) := by
1046	    show IsIso ((pushPullObjCongr F mIso).inv)
1047	    infer_instance
1048	  -- Target product reindex (via `whiskerEquiv`, keeping a clean lambda codomain so the projections
1049	  -- match the canonical comparisons): `∏_α (pushPull (legs∘e)) ≅ ∏_β (pushPull legs)`.
1050	  let prodIso : (∏ᶜ fun a => pushPullObj F (legs (e a))) ≅ ∏ᶜ fun b => pushPullObj F (legs b) :=
1051	    Pi.whiskerEquiv (f := fun a => pushPullObj F (legs (e a)))
1052	      (g := fun b => pushPullObj F (legs b)) e (fun a => Iso.refl _)
1053	  -- KEY identity (checked projection-by-projection over `α`): the canonical comparison for `legs`
1054	  -- transported back across the target reindex equals the slice-transported `α`-comparison.
1055	  have key : coprodToProdMap F legs ≫ prodIso.inv
1056	      = pushPullMap F mIso.hom ≫ coprodToProdMap F (fun a => legs (e a)) := by
1057	    show coprodToProdMap F legs ≫
1058	        (Pi.whiskerEquiv (f := fun a => pushPullObj F (legs (e a)))
1059	          (g := fun b => pushPullObj F (legs b)) e (fun a => Iso.refl _)).inv = _
1060	    refine Limits.Pi.hom_ext _ _ (fun a => ?_)
1061	    simp only [Category.assoc, Pi.whiskerEquiv_inv, Iso.refl_hom, Limits.Pi.map'_comp_π,
1062	      Category.comp_id, coprodToProdMap, Limits.Pi.lift_π]
1063	    rw [← pushPullMap_comp]
1064	    refine congrArg (fun g => pushPullMap F g) ?_

content:
degree-\(0\) component \(\varphi_0\) of \(\varphi\), there is an isomorphism of augmented
  cochain complexes
  \[
    C^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f \;\cong\; C'^\bullet.\operatorname{augment} f'.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  Define the isomorphism degreewise: take the degree-\(0\) component to be \(e_Y\) and the
  degree-\(n+1\) component to be \(\varphi_n\), the \(n\)-th component of \(\varphi\). The
  degree-\(0\) differential square is exactly the supplied compatibility square
  \(f \cdot \varphi_0 = e_Y \cdot f'\). For each degree \(n+1\), the augmented differential is the
  original differential of the base complex (the augmentation only modifies the bottom slot), so
  the square reduces to the commutativity condition of \(\varphi\) at degree \(n\).
  Hence all squares commute and the components assemble to an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Open-meet identity: intersection with \(V\) distributes over the cover meet]
  \label{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf}
  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
  Let \(\mathcal{U}\) be an open cover of \(X\), let \(V \subseteq X\) be an open, and let
  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I\) be a multi-index. Then, as opens of \(X\),
  \[
    \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V
      \;=\;
    \bigcap_{k}\bigl(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k) \cap V\bigr).
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  By definition \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma = \bigcap_k
  \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\), the \((p+1)\)-fold meet of the
  cover-member opens. The index type \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\) is nonempty, and in the frame of
  opens a binary meet distributes over a \emph{nonempty} indexed meet:
  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k A_k\bigr) \cap V = \bigcap_k (A_k \cap V)\). (Nonemptiness is essential:
  over the empty index the left side is \(\top \cap V = V\) while the right side is \(\top\).)
  Taking \(A_k = \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\) gives the claim.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Degreewise object iso of the non-augmented core comparison]
  \label{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coreIso_objIso}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\) and a degree \(p\). Write \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget}\cdot
  \operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) for the push--pull adapter and \(G_V =
  \operatorname{toPresheaf}\cdot\operatorname{ev}_V\) for the section-at-\(V\) functor. Then
  the degree-\(p\) terms of the evaluated non-augmented backbone complex and of the concrete
  restricted section {\v C}ech complex are isomorphic as abelian groups:
  \[
    \bigl((G_V\circ\Psi)\,\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},\mathcal{F})\bigr).X\,p
      \;\cong\;
    \bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}',\mathcal{F})\bigr).X\,p,
  \]
  i.e.\ \(\Gamma\bigl(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p\bigr) \cong
  \prod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1)\to I}
  \Gamma\bigl(\bigcap_k(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\cap V),\,
  \mathcal{F}\bigr)\), where \(\mathcal{U}'\) is the restricted family \(U'_i =
  \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i \cap V\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} supplies the evaluated push--pull product iso
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p) \cong \prod_\sigma
  \Gamma(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V, \mathcal{F})\). Post-compose
  with the product reindexing \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(\cdot, \mathcal{F})\) along the open-meet
  identity \(\operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V = \bigcap_k
  (\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\cap V)\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib} — factorwise the \(\operatorname{eqToIso}\) on the
  section group \(\Gamma(\cdot, \mathcal{F})\) obtained by applying \(\Gamma(\cdot, \mathcal{F})\)
  to that equality of opens. The composite lands on the degree-\(p\) term \(\prod_\sigma
  \Gamma(\bigcap_k(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\cap V), \mathcal{F})\) of
  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F})\), since for the restricted family
  \(U'_{\sigma k} = \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\cap V\) the section
  {\v C}ech term at \(\sigma\) is exactly \(\Gamma(\bigcap_k U'_{\sigma k}, \mathcal{F})\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[The core comparison intertwines the {\v C}ech differentials]
  \label{lem:coreIso_comm}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coreIso_comm}
  \uses{lem:coreIso_obj_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
    lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}, for each \(p\) the degreewise object
  isomorphisms \(\mathrm{objIso}\,p\) intertwine the two differentials:
  \[
    (\mathrm{objIso}\,p).\mathrm{hom} \cdot
      d^{p}_{\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U}')}
      \;=\;
    d^{p}_{(G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U})} \cdot
      (\mathrm{objIso}\,(p+1)).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Consequently \(\mathrm{coreIso} := \operatorname{isoOfComponents}(\mathrm{objIso},\,\cdot)\)
  is an isomorphism of cochain complexes \((G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
  \mathcal{F}) \cong \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F})\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:coreIso_obj_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
    lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  Read the section-complex differential \(d^p_{\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U}')}\) through the
  product equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}): by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} it is
  the alternating sum of presheaf face restrictions \(\sum_i (-1)^i\,
  \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\), each restriction being induced by the
  inclusion of the deleted-face intersection into the full intersection of the restricted opens
  \(U'_{\sigma k}\). The differential \(d^p_{(G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U})}\) is
  the evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech-nerve coface maps of the backbone complex, assembled
  from the same push--pull restriction comparisons. Under the degreewise identification of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso} — the evaluated push--pull product iso reindexed by the
  open-meet identity (Lemma~\ref{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}) — each coface of
  \((G_V\circ\Psi)\check{\mathcal{C}}(\mathcal{U})\) becomes the corresponding presheaf face
  restriction, because both are induced by the \emph{same} inclusion of intersection opens
  \(\bigcap_k(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,(\sigma k)\cap V)\). Hence the two alternating
  sums agree termwise and the square commutes; reusing the alternating-sum bookkeeping of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} discharges every degree at once.

  The degree-\(0\) compatibility datum \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} is the bottom-degree instance of this same match: the
  evaluated {\v C}ech augmentation \(G_V(\Psi(\operatorname{cechAugmentation}))\), read through
  the \(p=0\) object iso \(\mathrm{objIso}\,0\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso} at \(p=0\)), is
  the restriction-product map \(\varepsilon\colon t \mapsto (t|_{U'_i})_i\) on the nose, because
  both are induced by the inclusions \(\operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i \cap V
  \hookrightarrow V\). The prover therefore discharges \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\) alongside this
  lemma, as its \(p=0\) case, rather than as a separate obligation.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}
[Canonical augmentation of the section {\v C}ech complex over \(V\)]
  \label{def:sectionCechAugV}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAugV}
  \uses{lem:coreIso_obj_iso, def:cech_augmentation}
  \textit{Project-local.}
  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\). The \emph{canonical augmentation}
  \[
    \varepsilon_{\mathrm{can}} : \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \longrightarrow
      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})
  \]
  of the concrete section {\v C}ech complex over the restricted family
  \(U'_i = \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i \cap V\) is the evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech
  augmentation \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation},
  transported across the degree-\(0\) object isomorphism of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}.
  Concretely it is the composite
  \(G_V\bigl(\Psi(\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr) \cdot (\mathrm{objIso}\,0).\mathrm{hom}\),
  where \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget}\cdot\operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) is the
  push--pull adapter, \(G_V = \operatorname{toPresheaf}\cdot\operatorname{ev}_V\) is the
  section-at-\(V\) functor, and \(\mathrm{objIso}\,0\) is the degree-\(0\) instance of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso}. It carries no free parameter: this is the unique augmentation
  for which the comparisons below hold.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}
[The canonical section augmentation kills the first differential]
  \label{lem:sectionCechAugV_comp_d}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAugV_comp_d}
  \uses{def:sectionCechAugV, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d, lem:coreIso_comm}
  \textit{Project-local.}
  For the canonical augmentation \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{can}}\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:sectionCechAugV} the composite with the first section {\v C}ech
  differential vanishes,
  \[
    \varepsilon_{\mathrm{can}} \cdot d^0 \;=\; 0.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:sectionCechAugV, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d, lem:coreIso_comm}
  Conjugating \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{can}} \cdot d^0\) by the degree-\(0\) and degree-\(1\) object
  isomorphisms turns it, via the differential-match squares of Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_comm}, into
  the evaluation at \(V\) of the backbone augmentation composite
  \(G_V\bigl(\Psi(\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr) \cdot
  G_V\bigl(\Psi(d^0)\bigr)\). Since \(G_V\circ\Psi\) is a functor and the backbone identity
  \(\operatorname{cechAugmentation}\cdot d^0 = 0\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  holds, this composite is the functorial image of zero, hence zero; post-composing with the
  degree-\(1\) object iso preserves the vanishing. This is exactly the augmented-complex identity
  \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1\) read at \(V\) through the object isos.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

[The evaluated augmented {\v C}ech complex is the concrete section {\v C}ech complex]
  \label{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_complex_iso,
    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplexV}
  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The iso is to the AUGMENTED concrete section
  % complex D'_aug := (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε_can hε_can, NOT to the bare section
  % complex. The augmentation ε_can is the CANONICAL augmentation of def:sectionCechAugV — NOT a
  % free parameter: for a non-canonical ε the statement is false (the consumer hSec calls with no
  % free ε). The evaluated augmented complex D carries Γ(V,F) at its augmentation node
  % (def:cech_augmented_complex prepends F there), which the bare section complex does not have;
  % the two agree only after the section complex is augmented along the canonical ε_can. The
  % degree-0 compatibility datum h_compat is now DEFINITIONAL: ε_can is BY CONSTRUCTION the
  % evaluated Čech augmentation transported across coreIso_obj_iso 0, so the compatibility square
  % closes by definitional reduction rather than a diagram chase.
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmented_complex,
    def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d,
    def:sectionCechAugV, lem:sectionCechAugV_comp_d,
    lem:coreIso_obj_iso, lem:coreIso_comm, lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib,
    lem:mapHC_augment_iso, lem:map_augment_cond, lem:augmentCochainIso}
  Fix an open \(V \subseteq X\). The augmented {\v C}ech complex of
  Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmented_complex} evaluated at \(V\) (through
  \(\operatorname{toPresheaf}\)) --- call it \(D^\bullet\), the cochain complex of abelian
  groups whose augmentation node is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) and whose {\v C}ech degree-\(p\)
  term is \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) --- is isomorphic, as a
  cochain complex, to the \emph{augmented} concrete section {\v C}ech complex
  \[
    D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}} \;:=\;
    \bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F})\bigr).\operatorname{augment}
      \varepsilon\, h_\varepsilon
  \]
  built from the concrete section {\v C}ech complex over the restricted family
  \(U'_\sigma = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,\sigma \cap V\) (degree-\(p\) term
  \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), alternating coface restriction
  differential) by prepending the augmentation node \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the restriction product map \(\varepsilon : \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \to
      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\), \(t \mapsto \bigl(t|_{U_i \cap V}\bigr)_i\)
      --- the evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation
      \(\mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}; and
    \item the augmentation identity \(h_\varepsilon : \varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\), the
      evaluation at \(V\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}.
  \end{itemize}
  Concretely: at the augmentation node both complexes carry \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\),
  identified by the identity map; at {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p) \cong \prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}; and these degreewise maps
  intertwine the two differentials, including the augmentation differential \(\varepsilon\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d,
    lem:mapHC_augment_iso, lem:map_augment_cond, lem:augmentCochainIso,
    lem:coreIso_obj_iso, lem:coreIso_comm, lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}
  The augmentation node of both complexes is \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), matched by the
  identity. At {\v C}ech degree \(p\) the object isomorphism between
  \(\Gamma(V, \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p)\) and \(\prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma
  \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}. It remains to check the
  degreewise isomorphisms commute with the differentials.

  \emph{Augmentation bookkeeping (peeling the node).} Before matching differentials we strip the
  augmentation node off \(D^\bullet\). Writing \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget} \cdot
  \operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) for the push--pull adapter and \(G_V =
  \operatorname{toPresheaf} \cdot \operatorname{ev}_{V}\) for the section functor, the evaluated
  complex is \(D^\bullet = (G_V \circ \Psi)\bigl((\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet)
  .\operatorname{augment}\,(\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr)\). Applying
  Lemma~\ref{lem:mapHC_augment_iso} twice --- once for \(\Psi\) and once for \(G_V\), each an
  additive functor, with the augmentation condition transported by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:map_augment_cond} --- commutes both functors past
  \(\operatorname{augment}\), so \(D^\bullet \cong \bigl((G_V \circ \Psi)
  \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet\bigr).\operatorname{augment}\,\bigl((G_V \circ \Psi)
  (\operatorname{cechAugmentation})\bigr)\) with identity components. Lemma~\ref{lem:augmentCochainIso}
  then glues an iso of augmented complexes from three pieces: the \emph{non-augmented} core iso
  \[
    \mathrm{coreIso} : (G_V \circ \Psi)\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
      \mathcal{F})\bigr) \;\cong\; \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}', \mathcal{F}),
  \]
  the augmentation-object iso \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F}) \cong \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) (the
  identity \(e_Y\)), and the degree-\(0\) compatibility square \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\). This
  reduces the goal to the non-augmented \(\mathrm{coreIso}\), and the differential-match steps
  below apply to that reduced (non-augmented) goal --- not to the full augmented \(D^\bullet\),
  whose augmentation bookkeeping the three helper lemmas already discharge.

  \emph{The non-augmented core iso \(\mathrm{coreIso}\).} It is assembled componentwise from
  its degreewise object isos and a differential-match square. The degree-\(p\) object iso
  \(\mathrm{objIso}\,p : \Gamma(V,
  \operatorname{pushPullObj}\mathcal{F}\,Y_p) \cong \prod_\sigma \Gamma(U_\sigma \cap V,
  \mathcal{F})\) is Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_obj_iso} (the evaluated push--pull product iso
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso}, reindexed by the open-meet identity
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib}), and the differential-match square is
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_comm}.

  \emph{{\v C}ech differentials (degrees \(p \geq 0\)).} This is the content of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:coreIso_comm}: the differential of the section complex is, read through the
  product equivalence \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), the alternating sum of presheaf face
  restrictions \(\sum_i (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) computed in
  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}. The differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the
  evaluation at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech-nerve coface maps, assembled from the same push--pull
  restriction comparisons; under the degreewise identification each coface of \(D^\bullet\)
  becomes the corresponding face restriction, so the alternating sums agree. Reusing the
  alternating-sum bookkeeping of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} --- rather than
  rebuilding it --- matches these differentials.

  \emph{Augmentation differential.} The lowest differential of \(D^\bullet\) is the evaluation
  at \(V\) of the {\v C}ech augmentation \(\varepsilon : \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0\)
  (Definition~\ref{def:cech_augmentation}); read through the degree-\(0\) instance of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushPull_eval_prod_iso} it is exactly the restriction product map
  \(t \mapsto (t|_{U_i \cap V})_i\), which is the augmentation map \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) by construction. The compatibility square \(h_{\mathrm{compat}}\)
  fed to Lemma~\ref{lem:augmentCochainIso} is \emph{definitional up to the section-functor
  adapter}: once the adapter path \(\Psi = \operatorname{forget} \cdot
  \operatorname{restrictScalars}(\mathrm{id})\) followed by \(G_V = \operatorname{toPresheaf}
  \cdot \operatorname{ev}_{V}\) is unfolded, \(G_V(\Psi(\operatorname{cechAugmentation}))\)
  \emph{is} the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) on the nose, so the square closes by the
  identity / definitional reduction rather than a diagram chase --- it is precisely the
  degree-\(0\) instance of the differential match carried by \(\mathrm{coreIso}\). Both augmented
  complexes are well-formed by the shared identity \(\varepsilon \cdot d^0 = 0\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}). Hence the degreewise maps assemble to an
  isomorphism of cochain complexes \(D^\bullet \cong D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[The section {\v C}ech complex inside a cover member is contractible]
  \label{lem:cechSection_contractible}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_contractible}
  % NOTE: build target — augmented form. The contractible complex is the AUGMENTED section
  % complex D'_aug = (sectionCechComplex U'·).augment ε hε, NOT the bare section complex D'
  % (which is not contractible: a one-member cover {V} gives H⁰(D') = Γ(V,F) ≠ 0). The prover
  % re-signs the Lean declaration to Homotopy (𝟙 D'_aug) 0. The dep* engine supplies only the
  % positive-degree homotopy; the augmentation node is a separate explicit degree-0 computation,
  % given below.
  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex,
    def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cech_augmentation, lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the projection homotopy
  % $h(s)_{i_0\ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0\ldots i_p}$ contracting the extended
  % {\v C}ech complex) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L83--112).
  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact we will show that the extended complex localized
  % at $\mathfrak p$ is homotopic to zero.
  % There exists an index $i$ such that $f_i \not \in \mathfrak p$.
  % Choose and fix such an element $i_{\text{fix}}$. ...
  % Let us define a homotopy
  % $h : \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_{p + 1}}, \mathfrak p}
  % \longrightarrow \prod\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p} M_{f_{i_0} \ldots f_{i_p}, \mathfrak p}$
  % by the rule $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\text{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}$ ...
  % In other words, $h : \prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}, \mathfrak p} \to M_\mathfrak p$
  % is projection onto the factor
  % $M_{f_{i_{\text{fix}}}, \mathfrak p} = M_{\mathfrak p}$ ..."
  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof, the projection homotopy).}
  Suppose \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) for some fixed index
  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), i.e.\ \(V\) lies inside one cover member. Then the \emph{augmented}
  concrete section {\v C}ech complex \(D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso} --- the section {\v C}ech complex over the family
  \(U'_j = \operatorname{coverInterOpen}\mathcal{U}\,j \cap V\), augmented at
  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\) along the restriction product \(\varepsilon\) --- admits a
  contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:cech_acyclic_affine, lem:cech_engine_complex, def:cech_augmentation,
    lem:cech_augmentation_comp_d}
  Because \(V \le U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\), the restricted family \(U'_j = U_j \cap V\) has a
  \emph{maximum}: \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V = V\), and every
  \(U'_j \le V\). Consequently, for each multi-index \(\sigma\),
  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma} = U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap U_\sigma \cap V = U_\sigma \cap
  V = U'_\sigma\): prepending the index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) does not change the open, so the
  prepend map is the \emph{identity} on each coefficient \(\Gamma(U'_\sigma, \mathcal{F})\)
  (the membership criterion \(\operatorname{le\_coverInterOpen\_iff}\) of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_engine_complex}). The contracting homotopy is the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
  map, \(h(s)_\sigma = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}\,\sigma}\), exactly the projection homotopy of the
  Stacks proof; it splits into two parts.

  \emph{Positive {\v C}ech degrees \((p \ge 1\)).} On the section {\v C}ech part the
  dependent-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine --- \(\operatorname{depDiff}\),
  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\), \(\operatorname{depHomotopy\_spec}\),
  \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (here cited only as the
  Lean home of the \(\operatorname{dep\ast}\) engine declarations --- its standard-cover {\v
  C}ech-vanishing \emph{conclusion} is \emph{not} a mathematical dependency of the present
  argument) --- supplies the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) homotopy \(h\) with
  \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\), since its unit, shift, and coface-commutation
  compatibilities all hold with the prepend map acting as the identity on each coefficient.

  \emph{Augmentation node (the piece the engine does not supply).} The engine governs only the
  {\v C}ech degrees; the contracting-homotopy identity at the augmentation node must be checked
  directly. The relevant homotopy component runs from the lowest {\v C}ech term
  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\) down to the augmentation term
  \(\Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\), and is the \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)-th coordinate projection
  \(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} : (s_j)_j \mapsto s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}\) --- well defined because
  \(U'_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} = V\), so \(s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \in \Gamma(U_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} \cap V,
  \mathcal{F}) = \Gamma(V, \mathcal{F})\). We verify
  \(\varepsilon \circ \pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} + (\text{degree-}0\ \text{engine term}) = \mathrm{id}\)
  on \(s \in \prod_i \Gamma(U_i \cap V, \mathcal{F})\). The augmentation differential
  \(\varepsilon\) is the restriction product, so
  \(\bigl(\varepsilon(\pi_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}} s)\bigr)_j = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\); the
  degree-\(0\) contribution of the {\v C}ech homotopy supplies the complementary term
  \(s_j - s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}|_{U_j \cap V}\) coming from the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) shift on
  the {\v C}ech part. Their sum is \(s_j\), i.e.\ the identity. (This is the bottom node of the
  Stacks projection homotopy: \(h\) on \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to M\) is projection onto the
  factor \(M_{f_{i_{\mathrm{fix}}}} = M\).) Together the two parts give a homotopy
  \(\mathrm{id}_{D'^\bullet_{\mathrm{aug}}} \simeq 0\) of cochain complexes.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}
[Local vanishing of the evaluated augmented {\v C}ech homology]
  \label{lem:cechSection_isZero_homology}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechSection_isZero_homology}
  \uses{lem:cechSection_complex_iso, lem:cechSection_contractible,
    lem:isZero_homology_of_homotopy_id_zero}
  Let \(V \le \operatorname{coverOpen}\mathcal{U}\,i\) for some index \(i\). Then for every
  degree \(p\) the homology \(H^p(D^\bullet)\) of the augmented {\v C}ech complex evaluated at
  \(V\) (the complex \(D^\bullet\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:cechSection_complex_iso}) is a zero
  object; equivalently \(D^\bullet\) carries a contracting homotopy \(\mathrm{id}_{D^\bullet}
  \simeq 0\). This is the residual obligation of Step~3 of
  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution}.

136	namespace CombinatorialCech
137	
138	variable {ι : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] {n : ℕ}
139	
140	/-- Alternating coface (Čech) differential with constant coefficients in `M`:
141	`Cⁿ = (Fin n → ι) → M ⟶ Cⁿ⁺¹`, `(d t)(σ) = ∑ⱼ (-1)ʲ • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)`.
142	The argument `σ ∘ j.succAbove` is the `(n)`-tuple obtained from the `(n+1)`-tuple
143	`σ` by deleting the `j`-th index. -/
144	private def combDifferential (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M :=
145	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (n + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
146	
147	/-- The contracting homotopy of the localised complex: prepend the fixed
148	(globally invertible after localising at `s_r`) index `r`.
149	`(h u)(τ) = u (Fin.cons r τ)`. -/
150	private def combHomotopy (r : ι) (u : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) : (Fin n → ι) → M :=
151	  fun τ => u (Fin.cons r τ)
152	
153	@[simp] private lemma combHomotopy_zero (r : ι) :
154	    combHomotopy (M := M) (n := n) r 0 = 0 := by
155	  funext τ; simp [combHomotopy]
156	
157	/-- Composing `Fin.cons r` with the `(j+1)`-th coface map is `Fin.cons r` of the
158	`j`-th coface map: the bookkeeping identity behind the homotopy computation. -/
159	lemma cons_comp_succAbove_succ (r : ι) (σ : Fin (n + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (n + 1)) :
160	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove
161	      = Fin.cons r (σ ∘ k.succAbove) := by
162	  funext l
163	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ l
164	  · simp
165	  · intro i; simp [Fin.succ_succAbove_succ]
166	
167	/-- **Contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3; Stacks
168	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`):
169	`d ∘ h + h ∘ d = id` on `Cⁿ⁺¹`.  This is the alternating-sum cancellation: the
170	`j = 0` term of `h (d t)` is `t`, and the remaining terms cancel against `d (h t)`
171	in pairs of opposite sign. -/
172	private lemma combHomotopy_spec (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) :
173	    combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) + combHomotopy r (combDifferential t) = t := by
174	  funext σ
175	  simp only [combDifferential, combHomotopy, Pi.add_apply]
176	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun j : Fin (n + 2) =>
177	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ j.succAbove))]
178	  have h0 : (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (n + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (n + 2)).succAbove = σ := by
179	    funext i; simp
180	  rw [h0]
181	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
182	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
183	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
184	    rw [cons_comp_succAbove_succ, pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]; abel), add_zero]
185	
186	/-- Every cocycle is a coboundary in positive degree: if `d t = 0` then
187	`t = d (h t)`.  This is the homological content of `combHomotopy_spec` and is the
188	half (`ker d ⊆ im d`) carrying the geometric input. -/
189	private lemma combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle (r : ι) (t : (Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M)
190	    (ht : combDifferential t = 0) : combDifferential (combHomotopy r t) = t := by
191	  have h := combHomotopy_spec r t
192	  rw [ht, combHomotopy_zero, add_zero] at h
193	  exact h
194	
195	/-- Sign-cancellation behind `d² = 0`: under the index swap
196	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` the alternating sign flips. -/
197	private lemma combSign_flip (j : Fin (n + 2)) (i : Fin (n + 1)) :
198	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ)) * ((-1) ^ (i : ℕ))
199	      = - (((-1 : ℤ) ^ ((j.succAbove i : Fin (n + 2)) : ℕ))
200	            * ((-1) ^ ((i.predAbove j : Fin (n + 1)) : ℕ))) := by
201	  rcases lt_or_ge (i.castSucc) j with h | h
202	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_castSucc_lt _ _ h,
203	        Fin.val_castSucc, Fin.val_pred]
204	    have hpos : 0 < (j : ℕ) := lt_of_le_of_lt (Nat.zero_le _) (by exact_mod_cast h)
205	    obtain ⟨m, hm⟩ : ∃ m, (j : ℕ) = m + 1 := ⟨(j : ℕ) - 1, by omega⟩
206	    rw [hm]; simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel, pow_succ]; ring
207	  · rw [Fin.succAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h, Fin.predAbove_of_le_castSucc _ _ h,
208	        Fin.val_succ, Fin.coe_castPred]
209	    rw [pow_succ]; ring
210	
211	/-- **`d² = 0`** for the constant-coefficient alternating Čech complex.  Proved by
212	the standard sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` on
213	the double sum: the swap fixes the underlying composite coface
214	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`), is an involution
215	(`Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove`, `Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove`), has no
216	fixed point (`Fin.succAbove_ne`), and flips the sign (`combSign_flip`). -/
217	private lemma combDifferential_comp (t : (Fin n → ι) → M) :
218	    combDifferential (combDifferential t) = 0 := by
219	  funext σ
220	  simp only [combDifferential, Pi.zero_apply, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
221	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (n + 2) × Fin (n + 1) =>
222	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) • t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))]
223	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
224	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
225	    have harg : (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
226	        = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
227	      funext k
228	      simp only [Function.comp_apply]
229	      rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
230	    simp only [harg]
231	    rw [← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
232	    simp
233	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
234	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
235	    intro hj
236	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
237	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
238	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
239	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
240	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
241	
242	/-- **Positive-degree exactness** of the constant-coefficient Čech complex in the
243	`Function.Exact` form that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner L2) consumes node
244	by node.  Combines `combDifferential_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
245	`combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half).  Requires a
246	distinguished index `r : ι` — supplied, after localising at `s_r`, by the
247	spanning element itself. -/
248	private lemma combDifferential_exact (r : ι) (n : ℕ) :
249	    Function.Exact (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 1) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M))
250	      (combDifferential : ((Fin (n + 2) → ι) → M) → ((Fin (n + 3) → ι) → M)) := by
251	  intro x
252	  constructor
253	  · intro hx
254	    exact ⟨combHomotopy r x, combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle r x hx⟩
255	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
256	    exact combDifferential_comp y
257	
258	/-! ### Dependent-coefficient port (planner **L3 port**)
259	
260	The localised complex that `exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes
261	node-by-node has *varying* coefficients `M_{s_σ}` — the away localisation of `M`
262	at the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}` — not a single constant module `M`.  After
263	localising the whole extended complex at a spanning element `s_r`, the index `r`
264	is globally available and `s_r` becomes a unit, so the prepend
265	`σ ↦ Fin.cons r σ` is an *isomorphism* on the coefficients:
266	`M_{s_{cons r σ}} = M_{s_r · s_σ} ≅ M_{s_σ}` because `s_r` is already invertible.
267	
268	This section ports the constant-coefficient cancellation
269	(`combHomotopy_spec`, `combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle`) to that dependent
270	setting.  The away-localisation restriction maps are abstracted as additive
271	coface maps `δ` (`M_{s_{σ∘dⱼ}} → M_{s_σ}`, well defined because `s_σ` carries the
272	extra factor `s_{σ j}`) and the prepend isomorphisms as additive maps `c`
273	(`M_{s_{cons r σ}} → M_{s_σ}`).  The two compatibility identities below are
274	exactly the ones the away-localisation maps satisfy:
275	
276	* the **unit** identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` (deleting the prepended `r` from `cons r σ`
277	  recovers `σ`; on localisations the composite restriction is the identity since
278	  `s_r` is a unit), and
279	* the **shift** identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` (prepend commutes with the later
280	  cofaces), which is `cons_comp_succAbove_succ` lifted to the localisation maps.
281	
282	Threading the dependent family through the same alternating-sum cancellation as
283	the constant case then yields the dependent homotopy identity and the
284	cocycle⟹coboundary corollary that L2 consumes.  Constructing the actual `δ`/`c`
285	from `IsLocalizedModule.Away` (Mathlib `tilde` API:
286	`AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`, `IsLocalizedModule (.powers f)`) and the L1
287	identification of the abstract `CechComplex` terms with `∏_σ M_{s_σ}` remain the
288	outstanding bridge (see `CechAcyclic.affine`). -/
289	
290	section Dependent
291	
292	variable {A : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → ι) → Type*} [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)]
293	variable (r : ι)
294	variable (δ : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) → (j : Fin (m + 1)) →
295	    A m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ A (m + 1) σ)
296	variable (c : (m : ℕ) → (σ : Fin m → ι) → A (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) →+ A m σ)
297	
298	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
299	/-- Transport of a dependent cochain value along an equality of index tuples.
300	The transport friction that the constant-coefficient proof avoided (there every
301	coefficient is the same `M`) is isolated here. -/
302	lemma depTransport {m : ℕ} {x y : Fin m → ι} (h : x = y)
303	    (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) : h ▸ t x = t y := by
304	  subst h; rfl
305	
306	/-- Deleting the prepended index `r` (the `0`-th coface of `Fin.cons r σ`)
307	recovers `σ`. -/
308	lemma cons_comp_zero_succAbove {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → ι) :
309	    (Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 1)).succAbove = σ := by
310	  funext i; simp
311	
312	/-- Dependent (varying-coefficient) alternating Čech differential built from the
313	coface (localisation restriction) maps `δ`. -/
314	def depDiff {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
315	    ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ :=
316	  fun σ => ∑ j : Fin (m + 1), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (j : ℕ) • δ m σ j (t (σ ∘ j.succAbove))
317	
318	/-- Dependent contracting homotopy: prepend the distinguished index `r`, then
319	apply the prepend map `c`. -/
320	def depHomotopy {m : ℕ} (u : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) :
321	    ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ :=
322	  fun σ => c m σ (u (Fin.cons r σ))
323	
324	/-- **Dependent contracting-homotopy identity** (planner L3 port): `d ∘ h + h ∘ d
325	= id`, evaluated at a tuple `σ`.  Same alternating-sum cancellation as
326	`combHomotopy_spec`, threaded through the varying coefficients via the unit
327	identity `hu` (`c ∘ δ₀ = id`) and the shift identity `hsh`
328	(`c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c`). -/
329	lemma depHomotopy_spec
330	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
331	        (y : A (m + 1)
332	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
333	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
334	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
335	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
336	        (y : A (m + 1)
337	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
338	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
339	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
340	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
341	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ + depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = t σ := by
342	  simp only [depDiff, depHomotopy]
343	  rw [map_sum]
344	  simp only [map_zsmul]
345	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_succ (f := fun k : Fin (m + 2) =>
346	    (-1 : ℤ) ^ (k : ℕ) • c (m + 1) σ
347	      (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) k (t ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ k.succAbove))))]
348	  -- the `k = 0` term collapses to `t σ` via the unit identity
349	  rw [hu σ (t _)]
350	  rw [depTransport (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) t]
351	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_smul, Fin.val_succ]
352	  -- the `k = j.succ` terms cancel against the `d ∘ h` sum in pairs of opposite sign
353	  rw [add_left_comm, ← Finset.sum_add_distrib]
354	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_zero (fun x _ => by
355	    rw [hsh σ x (t _), depTransport (cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ x) t,
356	      pow_succ, mul_comm, neg_one_mul, neg_smul]
357	    abel), add_zero]
358	
359	/-- Dependent cocycle⟹coboundary (the geometric half consumed by L2): if
360	`depDiff t = 0` then `t = depDiff (depHomotopy t)`. -/
361	lemma depDiff_eq_of_cocycle
362	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
363	        (y : A (m + 1)
364	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
365	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
366	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
367	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
368	        (y : A (m + 1)
369	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
370	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
371	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
372	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι, A (m + 1) σ)
373	    (ht : depDiff δ t = 0) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) :
374	    depDiff δ (depHomotopy r c t) σ = t σ := by
375	  have h := depHomotopy_spec r δ c hu hsh t σ
376	  rw [show depHomotopy r c (depDiff δ t) σ = 0 by rw [ht]; simp [depHomotopy], add_zero] at h
377	  exact h
378	
379	omit [∀ m σ, AddCommGroup (A m σ)] in
380	/-- The composite coface tuple is symmetric under the `d²=0` index swap
381	`(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` (dependent analogue of the `harg` step
382	inside `combDifferential_comp`). -/
383	lemma comp_succAbove_swap {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι)
384	    (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1)) :
385	    (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) ∘ (i.predAbove j).succAbove
386	      = (σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove := by
387	  funext k
388	  simp only [Function.comp_apply]
389	  rw [Fin.succAbove_succAbove_succAbove_predAbove]
390	
391	/-- **Dependent `d² = 0`** for the varying-coefficient alternating Čech complex.
392	Same sign-reversing involution `(j, i) ↦ (j.succAbove i, i.predAbove j)` as
393	`combDifferential_comp`, with the coefficient transport handled by
394	`comp_succAbove_swap` and the coface-commutation hypothesis `hcomm` (the two
395	restriction maps into `M_{s_σ}` agree — a formal property of localisation maps,
396	independent of the choice of `r`). -/
397	lemma depDiff_comp
398	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
399	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
400	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
401	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
402	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
403	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
404	    {m : ℕ} (t : ∀ σ : Fin m → ι, A m σ) :
405	    depDiff δ (depDiff δ t) = 0 := by
406	  funext σ
407	  simp only [depDiff, Pi.zero_apply, map_sum, map_zsmul, Finset.smul_sum, smul_smul]
408	  rw [← Fintype.sum_prod_type (f := fun p : Fin (m + 2) × Fin (m + 1) =>
409	    ((-1 : ℤ) ^ (p.1 : ℕ) * (-1) ^ (p.2 : ℕ)) •
410	      δ (m + 1) σ p.1 (δ m (σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) p.2 (t ((σ ∘ p.1.succAbove) ∘ p.2.succAbove))))]
411	  apply Finset.sum_involution (fun p _ => (p.1.succAbove p.2, p.2.predAbove p.1))
412	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
413	    simp only []
414	    rw [hcomm σ j i (t _), depTransport (comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm t,
415	      ← add_smul, combSign_flip j i]
416	    simp
417	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _ _
418	    simp only [ne_eq, Prod.mk.injEq, not_and]
419	    intro hj
420	    exact absurd hj (Fin.succAbove_ne j i)
421	  · rintro ⟨j, i⟩ _
422	    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq]
423	    exact ⟨Fin.succAbove_succAbove_predAbove j i, Fin.predAbove_predAbove_succAbove j i⟩
424	  · intro a _; exact Finset.mem_univ _
425	
426	/-- **Dependent positive-degree exactness** in the `Function.Exact` form that
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (planner **L2**) consumes after localising at a
428	spanning element `s_r`.  Combines `depDiff_comp` (`im ⊆ ker`) with
429	`depDiff_eq_of_cocycle` (`ker ⊆ im`, the homotopy half carrying the
430	`s_r`-invertibility input).  This is the dependent-coefficient analogue of
431	`combDifferential_exact`. -/
432	lemma depDiff_exact
433	    (hu : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι)
434	        (y : A (m + 1)
435	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)),
436	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) 0 y)
437	          = (cons_comp_zero_succAbove r σ) ▸ y)
438	    (hsh : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → ι) (k : Fin (m + 1))
439	        (y : A (m + 1)
440	          ((Fin.cons r σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)),
441	        c (m + 1) σ (δ (m + 1) (Fin.cons r σ) (k.succ) y)
442	          = δ m σ k (c m (σ ∘ k.succAbove) ((cons_comp_succAbove_succ r σ k) ▸ y)))
443	    (hcomm : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 2) → ι) (j : Fin (m + 2)) (i : Fin (m + 1))
444	        (z : A m ((σ ∘ j.succAbove) ∘ i.succAbove)),
445	        δ (m + 1) σ j (δ m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) i z)
446	          = δ (m + 1) σ (j.succAbove i)
447	              (δ m (σ ∘ (j.succAbove i).succAbove) (i.predAbove j)
448	                ((comp_succAbove_swap σ j i).symm ▸ z)))
449	    (m : ℕ) :
450	    Function.Exact (depDiff δ (m := m + 1)) (depDiff δ (m := m + 2)) := by
451	  intro x
452	  constructor
453	  · intro hx
454	    exact ⟨depHomotopy r c x,
455	      funext fun σ => depDiff_eq_of_cocycle r δ c hu hsh x hx σ⟩
456	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
457	    exact depDiff_comp δ hcomm y
458	
459	end Dependent
460	
461	end CombinatorialCech

463	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — away-localisation comparison maps
464	
465	The concrete `δ` (coface) and `c` (prepend) maps that the dependent combinatorial

input.description: Find section Čech defs in PresheafCech.lean

content:
13:- `sectionCechComplex`        — cosimplicial `Ab`-valued section Čech complex
14:                                (`\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechComplex}`)
43:Step 1 — `sectionCechComplex`
57:`sectionCechComplex` is a plain cochain complex of `R`-modules where `R = O_X(U)`.
303:noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
328:obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
334:noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
336:  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)

240	
241	Project-local because Mathlib packages the two halves separately but not their
242	composite as the corepresentability of evaluation by `free(yoneda V)`. The target
243	`(F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (op V)` is the underlying set of the section group
244	`F(V)`. -/
245	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
246	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
247	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃
248	      (F.presheaf ⋙ forget Ab).obj (Opposite.op V) :=
249	  PresheafOfModules.freeHomEquiv.trans yonedaEquiv
250	
251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

1484	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — quasi-coherent sections as away localisations (L1, step (b))
1485	
1486	The categorical→module bridge needs the section-identification of
1487	`def:qcoh_sections_localized`: over a basic open `D(g)` the sections of a
1488	quasi-coherent sheaf are the away localisation `M_g`, and the restriction maps
1489	between basic opens are the canonical localisation comparison maps.  For the
1490	standard sheaf `tilde M` this is *verbatim* from Mathlib's `Tilde` development
1491	(`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen` carries `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g)` and the
1492	restriction compatibility is `tilde.toOpen_res`); the only project-local content is
1493	(i) the multi-index intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` identification, which lets
1494	the degree-`p` section group over the `(p+1)`-fold intersection be read as the
1495	localisation `M_{s_σ}` (this is what `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes
1496	degreewise), and (ii) the identification of the abstract presheaf restriction with
1497	`AwayComparison.comparison`, the differential brick.
1498	
1499	For an *arbitrary* quasi-coherent `F` the remaining input is the affine equivalence
1500	`F ≅ tilde(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8); see `def:qcoh_sections_localized`.  The tilde case
1501	below is the gap-free part that lands the named target. -/
1502	
1503	/-- Splitting an indexed infimum over `Fin (n+1)` into the `0`-th term and the
1504	infimum over the tail.  Lattice-combinatorial helper behind `basicOpen_sprod`. -/
1505	private lemma iInf_fin_succ {α : Type*} [CompleteLattice α] (n : ℕ) (f : Fin (n + 1) → α) :
1506	    (⨅ i, f i) = f 0 ⊓ ⨅ i : Fin n, f i.succ := by
1507	  apply le_antisymm
1508	  · exact le_inf (iInf_le _ 0) (le_iInf fun i => iInf_le _ i.succ)
1509	  · refine le_iInf fun i => ?_
1510	    refine Fin.cases inf_le_left (fun j => le_trans inf_le_right (iInf_le _ j)) i
1511	
1512	/-- **Multi-index basic-open intersection** (geometric input to step (c)): the
1513	`(p+1)`-fold intersection of the basic opens `D(s_{σ k})` of a {\v C}ech multi-index
1514	`σ : Fin n → ι` is the basic open `D(s_σ)` of the product `s_σ = ∏ₖ s_{σ k}`.  This
1515	identifies the section group `F(⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}))` of `sectionCechCosimplicial` with the
1516	away localisation `M_{s_σ}` of `SectionCechModule.dCoeff`. -/
1517	lemma basicOpen_sprod {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u} (n : ℕ) (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1518	    (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)) : (Spec R).Opens)
1519	      = PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (∏ k, s (σ k)) := by
1520	  induction n with
1521	  | zero =>
1522	      rw [show (∏ k, s (σ k)) = 1 from Finset.prod_of_isEmpty _]
1523	      simp only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]
1524	      exact iInf_of_empty _
1525	  | succ m ih =>
1526	      rw [Fin.prod_univ_succ, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_mul, ← ih (fun i => σ i.succ),
1527	        iInf_fin_succ]
1528	      rfl
1529	
1530	/-- **Quasi-coherent sections over a {\v C}ech intersection are an away localisation**
1531	(`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, tilde case — step (b) of the L1 bridge).  For the
1532	standard sheaf `tilde M` of an `R`-module `M`, the section-restriction map from `M`
1533	to the sections over the `(p+1)`-fold basic-open intersection
1534	`⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k}) = D(s_σ)` exhibits that section group as the away localisation
1535	`M_{s_σ}` (`IsLocalizedModule` for `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`).  Combines the
1536	Mathlib instance `IsLocalizedModule (.powers g) (tilde.toOpen M (D g)).hom` with the
1537	intersection identification `basicOpen_sprod`.  This is the degreewise section
1538	identification that `lem:section_cech_homology_exact` consumes; the restriction
1539	compatibility (item (5) of the blueprint definition) is `tilde.toOpen_res` together
1540	with `qcohRestriction_eq_comparison` below. -/
1541	lemma qcohSectionsAwayLocalized {R : CommRingCat.{u}} {ι : Type u}
1542	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {n : ℕ} (s : ι → R) (σ : Fin n → ι) :
1543	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
1544	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom := by
1545	  rw [basicOpen_sprod]
1546	  infer_instance
1547	
1548	/-- **Restriction = localisation comparison** (`def:qcoh_sections_localized`, item (5);
1549	the differential brick of step (c)).  For the standard sheaf `tilde M`, the presheaf
1550	restriction map between basic-open section groups `M_a → M_b` (along an inclusion
1551	`D(b) ⊆ D(a)`) is, as an `R`-linear map, the canonical away-localisation comparison
1552	`AwayComparison.comparison` — provided `a` acts invertibly on `M_b` (`Inverts a M_b`,
1553	which holds whenever `a ∣ b`, the {\v C}ech-face case).  Proved by the universal
1554	property `AwayComparison.comparison_unique`: both the restriction and the comparison
1555	are `R`-linear maps that recover `tilde.toOpen M (D b)` after precomposition with
1556	`tilde.toOpen M (D a)` (the restriction does so by `tilde.toOpen_res`).  Summed over
1557	the alternating signs, this identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with the
1558	module differential `SectionCechModule.dDiff`. -/
1559	lemma qcohRestriction_eq_comparison {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {a b : R}
1560	    (i : (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b : (Spec R).Opens) ⟶ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1561	    (hb : AwayComparison.Inverts a
1562	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
1563	        (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)))) :
1564	    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map i.op).hom
1565	      = AwayComparison.comparison (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1566	          (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb := by
1567	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
1568	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom := inferInstance
1569	  refine (AwayComparison.comparison_unique
1570	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom
1571	    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom hb _ ?_).symm
1572	  have h := AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toOpen_res M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
1573	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b) i
1574	  exact congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom h
1575	
1576	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech homology bridge (L1 steps c, d)
1577	
1578	The categorical→module bridge of `lem:section_cech_homology_exact`: the
1579	`Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex `sectionCechComplex` (of `PresheafCech.lean`)
1580	has its degree-`p` object a *categorical product* `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` in `Ab`, and
1581	its differential the alternating sum of the {\v C}ech coface restrictions.  These
1582	lemmas (c1)–(c3) move that abstract complex to the concrete localised-module complex
1583	`SectionCechModule.dDiff` (whose positive-degree exactness `dDiff_exact` is step (a)),
1584	and read off homology vanishing. -/
1585	
1586	section SectionCechBridge
1587	
1588	open CategoryTheory.Limits AlgebraicTopology
1589	
1590	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
1591	
1592	/-- **(c1) Element-level product equivalence** (`lem:section_cech_product_equiv`):
1593	the underlying type of the degree-`p` object `∏ᶜ_σ F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of the section
1594	{\v C}ech cosimplicial object is the dependent product `∏_σ ToType (F(⨅ₖ U (σ k)))`.
1595	Supplied by `CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv` (the forgetful functor of
1596	`Ab` preserves discrete products). -/
1597	noncomputable def sectionCechProductEquiv {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1598	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ) :
1599	    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≃
1600	      (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, ToType (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))) :=
1601	  Concrete.productEquiv
1602	    (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
1603	
1604	/-- Coordinate projection of `sectionCechProductEquiv`: the `σ`-component is the
1605	underlying group map of the categorical projection `Pi.π … σ`.  A definitional
1606	restatement of `Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply`, named so that downstream proofs can
1607	`rw` it without fighting the `Concrete.productEquiv` coercion. -/
1608	lemma sectionCechProductEquiv_apply {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1609	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) (p : ℕ)
1610	    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial U F).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
1611	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
1612	    sectionCechProductEquiv U F p y σ
1613	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
1614	          (Pi.π (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k)))) σ) y :=
1615	  Concrete.productEquiv_apply_apply _ y σ
